mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force rapid equity repricing, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns do not turn negative following INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism shows no predictive edge compared to a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets absorb CPIAUCSL readings above 2.5% without generating negative 20-day returns, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 50% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product establishes the baseline economic momentum that directly translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 60% of positive growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production alongside rising inflation indicates stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months while CPIAUCSL rises
failure_condition: The stagflation signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in over 55% of triggered instances, or the mechanism loses predictive power when inflation remains below 2%.
